places.name: Livraria Lello
places.description: One of the most beautiful bookstores in the world hides a neo-Gothic interior behind an art nouveau facade.

places.name: Igreja de São Francisco
places.description: The stunning interior of this Gothic church is covered in ornate gold details.

places.name: McDonald's Imperial
places.description: This restored Portuguese cafe may well be the most beautiful place in the world to grab a Big Mac.

places.name: Carmo and Carmelitas Churches
places.description: A hidden three-foot-wide house separates these two regal churches.

places.name: Igreja de São Francisco Catacombs
places.description: Ancient catacombs form the foundation for this Rococo orgy of a church.

places.name: Sao Bento Station
places.description: Elaborate tile work in this railway station tells the story of Portugal.

places.name: Jardim Botânico do Porto (Porto Botanical Garden)
places.description: A small but charming botanical garden that contains many exotic plant species.

places.name: McDonald's Imperial
places.description: This restored Portuguese cafe may well be the most beautiful place in the world to grab a Big Mac.

places.name: Semea
places.description: Rabanadas, the Portuguese answer to French toast, are reborn here with a glassy sugar crust and a center as light as whipped cream.

places.name: A Regaleira
places.description: The original version of Porto's most famous sandwich is just as good here as it was a century ago.
